ocr_mistral.text:
He directs that the sum of $30,000 shall be laid out in the purchase of land in the State of Ohio for the use of the Compt's his said slaves and that the said land should be allotted equally among them giving them their lots equally together according to their families. The residue of the fund he directed to be laid out in providing for the removal of his said slaves to Ohio and then in equal portions among them in fixing them up for housekeeping such as putting up houses where necessary for them purchasing horses stock furniture plantation utensils & clothing and if any sureties should remain, the same should be equally divided in cash among them, paying to the parents, where the children are under 21 yrs of age the share of such children.

This will was made in 1839 and the testator and till 1859 at which time the number of his slaves and the value of his real & personal property particularly the latter had very much increased.

Your compts, and those for whom they sue were the slaves of the said John Fox, were emancipated by his will, and are the sole beneficiaries and devises under the will of the whole estate which was to be disposed of for their benefit as therein set out.

The estate was appraised, and

The residue of the fund he directed to be laid out in providing for the removal of his said slaves to Ohio and then in equal portions among them in fixing them up for housekeeping such as putting up houses where necessary for them purchasing horses stock furniture plantation utensils & clothing and if any sureties should remain, the same should be equally divided in cash among them, paying to the parents, where the children are under 21 yrs of age the share of such children.

This will was made in 1839 and the testator and till 1859 at which time the number of his slaves and the value of his real & personal property particularly the latter had very much increased.

Your compts, and those for whom they sue were the slaves of the said John Fox, were emancipated by his will, and are the sole beneficiaries and devises under the will of the whole estate which was to be disposed of for their benefit as therein set out.

transcribe_handwriting.text:
He directs that the sum of $30,000 shall be
laid out in the purchase of land in the
state of Ohio, for the use of the Compt's his
said slaves and that the said land should
be allotted equally among them giving
them their lots equally together according
to their families. The residue of the
funds he directed to be laid out in
providing for the removal of his said slaves
to Ohio and then in equal portions
among them in fitting them up
for housekeeping such as putting
up houses, where necessary for them
purchasing horses stock furniture
plantation utensils & clothing and
if any surplus should remain, the
same should be equally divided
in cash among them, paying, to the
parents, where the children are under
21 yrs age, the share of such children.

This will was made in 1839 and the testator
lived till 1859 at which time the number
of his slaves and the value of his real
& personal property particularly the
latter had very much increased.

Your compts, and those for whom
they sue, were the slaves of the said
John Fox, were emancipated by his
will, and are the sole beneficiaries
and devises, under the will of the
whole estate which was to be disposed
of for their benefit as therein set out.

The estate was appraised, and
